blocks.body: Present Perfect — have / has + V3 for a result now, life experience, recent actions, and time that continues up to the present.

{"level":"A2–B1","intro":"Present Perfect connects the past with the present: the action happened before, but its result, experience, or time period matters now.","rules":[{"label":"Forms","color":"emerald","text":"Start with the model have / has + V3.","example":"I have finished. / She has gone home."},{"label":"Result now","color":"blue","text":"Use this tense when a past action has a result in the present moment.","example":"I have lost my keys. / We have cleaned the room."},{"label":"Life experience","color":"amber","text":"The tense is often used for experience without a specific past time.","example":"Have you ever been to Italy? / He has never tried sushi."},{"label":"Time expressions","color":"violet","text":"Words like just, already, yet, ever, never, for, since often point to Present Perfect.","example":"She has just arrived. / I have lived here for five years."}]}

{"title":"1. Lessons in order","intro":"Start with the form, then move to negatives, questions, and time expressions.","items":[{"label":"A2–B1","title":"Forms and Use","subtitle":"have / has + V3 and the main meanings of the tense."},{"label":"A2–B1","title":"Negatives","subtitle":"haven't / hasn't + V3 and common patterns with yet or never."},{"label":"A2–B1","title":"Questions and Short Answers","subtitle":"Have you...?, Has she...? and short answers."},{"label":"A2–B1","title":"Time Expressions","subtitle":"just, already, yet, ever, never, for, since."}]}

{"title":"2. Where you need this tense","sections":[{"label":"Result now","color":"emerald","description":"Show a past action whose result matters in the present moment.","examples":[{"en":"I have broken my glasses.","ua":"The result matters now."},{"en":"She has finished her homework.","ua":"The task is complete now."}]},{"label":"Life experience","color":"blue","description":"Talk about life experience without giving an exact past time.","examples":[{"en":"We have visited Prague twice.","ua":"Life experience up to now."},{"en":"He has never flown before.","ua":"No experience before now."}]},{"label":"Recent actions","color":"amber","description":"Show an event that happened recently and still feels current.","examples":[{"en":"They have just arrived.","ua":"A very recent action."},{"en":"I have already sent the email.","ua":"The action is complete before now."}]},{"label":"Unfinished time up to now","color":"violet","description":"Use Present Perfect for time that started in the past and continues up to now.","examples":[{"en":"I have lived here since 2020.","ua":"The situation continues now."},{"en":"She has worked here for six months.","ua":"A period from the past until now."}]}]}

{"title":"3. In short","items":["Present Perfect = have / has + V3.","This tense connects a past action with a present result or experience.","It is often used with just, already, yet, ever, never, for, and since.","After the four basic tenses, it is the natural next step in the Tenses block."]}
